should_trigger: Design a control loop for my multi-agent orchestrator so it stops oscillating between retry and rollback when tests are flaky.
Our execution agent gets delayed verifier feedback and keeps over-correcting. Use engineering cybernetics to stabilize the runtime.
Model this auto-orchestrator as planner, worker, tool, verifier, and feedback blocks, then suggest stability and noise-filtering checks.
How should an orchestrator adaptively search for the best model/tool configuration without wasting too much budget on probing?
should_not_trigger: Summarize the history of cybernetics and Norbert Wiener for a literature review.
Create a normal project plan for writing a marketing website with tasks and deadlines.
Explain how to implement a REST endpoint in Go without any feedback-control or runtime orchestration question.
edge_cases: The orchestrator has retries, queue limits, and human approval gates, but I only need a quick diagnosis of whether the repeated failures are noise, delay, or a bad control policy.
